commit.message:
fix: link validity + full i18n coverage across all pages Two issues reported by audit: ── Broken links ──────────────────────────────────────────── * Footer "Changelog" link pointed to CHANGELOG.md which does not exist in the repo — 404 on every page. Replaced with a "Commit history" link pointing to /commits/main, which IS valid and is the de-facto changelog. * Footer i18n key renamed footer_changelog → footer_commits and localised in all 5 languages (DE/FR/IT/RM/EN) across all 5 pages (index, entscheide, gesetze, mcp, ueber). ── Hero wording ──────────────────────────────────────────── Per user: drop "complete"/"vollständig" from the hero headline and speak of "court decisions", not "case law": de: "Der offene Datensatz Schweizer Gerichtsentscheide." fr: "Le corpus ouvert des décisions judiciaires suisses." it: "Il corpus aperto delle decisioni giudiziarie svizzere." rm: "Il corpus avert da las decisiuns giudicialas svizras." en: "The open dataset of Swiss court decisions." ── Translation completeness — the About page was broken ──── The /ueber/ page had 19 data-t keys in markup but ~30 hardcoded German paragraphs, headings and list items that never translated when the user switched languages — the entire body stayed German. Audited every ,

,

  • on every page; wrapped each hardcoded element in a data-t span and added the full 5-language translation set. Results of final audit: ✓ docs/index.html — 74 data-t keys, 0 hardcoded German ✓ docs/entscheide/ — 79 keys, 0 hardcoded ✓ docs/gesetze/ — 31 keys, 0 hardcoded (+10 new keys) ✓ docs/mcp/ — 31 keys, 0 hardcoded (+10 new keys) ✓ docs/ueber/ — 50 keys, 0 hardcoded (+31 new keys) All data-t keys defined in all 5 languages on all 5 pages. ── About page content refresh ────────────────────────────── While translating, also updated the stale Zahlen section: 80 → 5'000+ federal laws 33'000+ cantonal (static) → 30'000+ (reflects the new LexFind mirror) And the "Gesetze" methodology bullet now reflects the local cantonal mirror instead of the old "LexFind JSON-API with tiered caches" description. No other behavioural changes — just data-t attributes, label text, and new i18n entries. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: ONE SOURCE OF TRUTH — workflows, model detection, architectures

Major consolidation into spellcaster_core/:

1. guild_common.py: eliminated 140-line fallback duplication. Now a thin
   wrapper that imports from spellcaster_core.model_detect. Searches
   multiple locations (dev checkout, GIMP bundle, packaged build).

2. server.py: imports architectures from spellcaster_core.architectures
   directly (not _architectures shim). Finds spellcaster_core from
   comfyui-spellcaster/ (dev) or plugins/gimp/.../spellcaster_core (bundle).

3. _workflows_v2.py: now a shim that imports from spellcaster_core.workflows.
   The canonical 4083-line workflows.py lives in spellcaster_core/.
   Bundled copy in plugins/gimp/comfyui-connector/spellcaster_core/.

4. build_nsfw.py: _ensure_spellcaster_core() copies canonical source
   into GIMP plugin dir during staging, before patches apply.

5. dev/sync_check.py + dev/sync_core.py: verification and propagation
   tools for keeping spellcaster_core identical across all locations.

Dependency graph (before):
  server.py -> plugins/gimp/.../_architectures.py (full 33KB file)
  server.py -> plugins/gimp/.../_workflows_v2.py (full 196KB file)
  guild_common.py -> 140 lines of duplicated model detection

Dependency graph (after):
  server.py -> spellcaster_core.architectures (canonical)
  server.py -> spellcaster_core.workflows (via shim)
  guild_common.py -> spellcaster_core.model_detect (canonical)
  All shims -> spellcaster_core/* (bundled or dev checkout)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v0.7.3 — fix text toolbar clipping (colors + delete now work)

User reported: selected text shows selection + handles but neither
the color swatches nor any way to remove the text are visible. Two
related bugs:

1. #tcTextToolbar was positioned absolute inside .tc-stage. The
   stage has overflow: hidden for canvas clipping, so the toolbar
   was getting cut off whenever the selected text was near any
   stage edge — which covered most real-world placements.

   Fix: toolbar is now position: fixed + z-index: 11000.
   TextToolbar.updatePosition() computes viewport coords
   (stage.getBoundingClientRect() + stage-local offset) so the
   toolbar is never inside the clipped region.

2. Redundant canvas-native red ✕ delete button on the top-right
   of any selected text overlay. Drawn by TextOverlays.drawAll
   with a shadow and stored on item._deleteBtn (x, y, r).
   Drag._onDown hit-tests this button FIRST, before normal
   drag/resize logic — a click inside it removes the overlay
   and shows a confirmation toast.

   Belt-and-suspenders: even if the HTML toolbar somehow still
   fails (browser-specific z-index weirdness, parent clipping we
   haven't considered, etc), the canvas button is drawn into the
   same canvas the user is looking at and CANNOT be hidden.

Verified in the preview harness with a text overlay placed near
the right edge at (1700, 80):
- toolbar position:fixed, z-index: 11000, left: 1257 px,
  top: 184 px, visibleRect has width (no longer clipped)
- _deleteBtn cached at (2083, 56, r: 26)
- Click on canvas ✕ via stage mousedown → 1 → 0 overlays
- Swatch click sets item.color = '#fbbf24'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Logo : uniquement la version claire sur toutes les pages

- Suppression du logo foncé des HTML
- CSS simplifié : plus de logique dark/light, juste .logo-img

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add message sending from dashboard with flicker-free reloads

Add send message UI and API endpoint for the dashboard Messages tab.
Messages reload smoothly after send/refresh without clearing the list,
and auto-scroll to the latest message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Windows service: never give up retrying after failure

Previously the failure actions only had 3 retries (10s, 30s, 60s) and
then stopped. Changed so the 3rd action is 5 minutes (300s), and since
sc.exe repeats the last action for all subsequent failures, the service
will retry every 5 minutes forever.

Also added failureflag=1 so failure actions trigger on clean exits too,
not just crashes — matches Linux systemd Restart=always behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 1: XRPL testnet RLUSD payments working

- XRPL client singleton (testnet WSS connection)
- Wallet funding via testnet faucet (issuer, owner, vault, payee)
- RLUSD token: hex currency code, trustline setup, DefaultRipple on issuer
- Payment flow: issuer→owner→vault→payee tested and confirmed on testnet
- Setup script: creates 4 wallets, trustlines, distributes RLUSD, saves .env
- Test script: vault→payee 5 RLUSD payment with balance verification

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use :global() for all MDX content selectors — scoped CSS was never applying

Astro adds data-astro-cid to elements rendered in the template, but NOT
to elements rendered via <Content /> (MDX). All .guia-content child rules
(figure, p, h2, blockquote, hr, table etc.) required [data-astro-cid] on
the element itself, so none of them ever matched MDX-rendered content.

Fix: wrap all MDX child selectors in :global() so Astro omits the
attribute from those parts of the selector. This correctly targets all
HTML elements produced by the MDX renderer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v0.7.2 — make "remove video" discoverable

User feedback: "no way to remove a video". The ✕ button was always
there in the Active Sources list, but it was muted-grey with a
transparent border and only turned red on hover — invisible when
scanning the UI.

Three fixes:

1. .tc-src-remove now has a permanent red tint (rgba(239,68,68,.14)
   background, rgba(239,68,68,.4) border, #fca5a5 text, 700 weight,
   bigger padding). Unmissable.

2. Delete/Backspace key now removes the last-clicked video source
   (Drag.selectedSourceId). Complements the existing Delete-on-
   text-overlay path. onDown hit-test flags the clicked source as
   selected.

3. One-time hint toast on the first source added:
   "💡 Glisse pour déplacer · coin pour redimensionner · ✕ dans
   la liste ou touche Suppr pour retirer" (and en/ar variants).
   Tells the user every interaction path in one line, 5.5s
   duration.

Also: Engine.removeSource now clears Drag.selectedSourceId if it
matched, and emits a confirmation toast "✕ Source retirée: <label>".

Verified in the preview harness:
- Remove button: red rgba(239,68,68,.14) background, bold, present ✓
- Title reads "✕ Retirer"
- Delete key on selected source: 1 → 0 sources, selectedSourceId
  cleared ✓
- Button click path still works: 1 → 0 sources ✓
- 3 new i18n keys × 3 langs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix install instructions: edit config AFTER install, not before

Previously the README told users to edit nutclient.json in the extracted
folder before running install.sh, but install.sh actually copies
nutclient.json.linux-example to /opt/nutclient/nutclient.json — so
pre-install edits to nutclient.json were silently ignored.

New flow (matches what install scripts actually do):
1. Run install script
2. Edit /opt/nutclient/nutclient.json (or C:\NutClient\nutclient.json)
3. Start the service manually the first time

Also added a "Modifying Settings After Install" section showing how to
edit the config and restart the service later, with notes on log
locations for troubleshooting parse errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add intraday decomposition: 5-min path generation from daily bars

- Intraday data fetcher (yfinance 5m/1m + Alpaca API support)
- Pattern calibrator: extracts U-shaped vol, J-shaped volume, overnight
  gap distribution, intraday autocorrelation from real 5-min data
- Intraday generator: decomposes each daily bar into 78 realistic 5-min
  bars preserving vol seasonality, mean-reversion, fat tails, and exact
  daily return constraint
- Falls back to stylized defaults when no intraday data available
- Full UI tab with price paths, vol/volume profiles, gap stats, export

commit.message:
fix(ci): read version from recce/VERSION for manual nightly UI release

When manually triggering a Nightly release of @datarecce/ui without
specifying a version, the workflow read from package.json (hardcoded
0.2.1) instead of recce/VERSION. This produced incorrect nightly
versions like 0.2.1-nightly.YYYYMMDD.

Read from recce/VERSION and strip the .devN suffix to derive the base
semver, matching the behavior of the automated nightly path.

Closes DRC-3214

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Kent Huang <kent@infuseai.io>

commit.message:
feat(framework-elevation): P1-P9 elevation completa

P1 — Fachada de pagamento em web/views/payment.py
  Todas as leituras de status via payment_svc.get_payment_status(order).
  payment.get("status") removido das três views (PaymentView, PaymentStatusView,
  MockPaymentConfirmView). order.data["payment"] restrito a dados de exibição/auditoria.

P2 — Invariante split-statement em test_invariants.py
  _scan_split_payment_status() varre arquivos linha a linha, rastreia variáveis
  atribuídas de .data.get("payment") e sinaliza qualquer .get("status") posterior.
  Dois novos testes: views/ e handlers/.

P3 — Docs sincronizados com o runtime
  architecture.md: nomes de apps corrigidos (offering→offerman, etc.),
  CustomerEnsureHandler removido do diagrama de fluxo, tabela de namespaces adicionada.
  lifecycle.md: handlers síncronos (customer.ensure, stock.hold, fulfillment.create)
  documentados como chamadas diretas, não Directives.

P4 — Modelo de governança de regras documentado
  rules/engine.py: docstring abrangente explicando a separação em dois níveis
  (handlers estáticos vs regras dinâmicas no DB).
  docs/reference/rules.md: documentação completa com diagrama de boot, tabela de
  validators/modifiers disponíveis, instruções para registrar novas regras.

P5 — Testes de integridade de holds de estoque
  test_stock_hold_integrity.py: 5 classes, 10 testes cobrindo qtd correta,
  expansão de bundle, estoque parcial, verify_holds e adoção de holds de sessão.

P7 — Fachadas de fulfillment em services/fulfillment.py
  get_fulfillment_status(order) e get_tracking_state(order) expostos como API
  pública do serviço. Views de rastreamento podem consumir via fachada.

P8 — data-schemas.md corrigido e classificado
  "status": "captured" removido do exemplo JSON de Order.data (contrato errado).
  Coluna "Classe" adicionada à tabela de payment (canonical/display/audit).
  Nota explícita: status de pagamento nunca em order.data.
  Referências obsoletas (customer.ensure directive, flows._check_availability) removidas.

P9 — Suite de conformidade cross-package
  test_conformance.py: 14 cenários canônicos de lifecycle (C-01 a C-14) cobrindo
  PIX/card, delivery/pickup, confirmação, cancelamento, retorno com estorno,
  reabastecimento com WO e fiscal. Infraestrutura compartilhada com _PATCHES e
  _partial_patches para testes que precisam do stock.hold real.

Fixes de bootstrap:
  apps.py: register_active_rules() envolvido em try/except OperationalError para
  evitar falha durante migrate inicial.
  checks.py: check_fiscal_adapter() idem para Channel.objects.all().

commit.message:
fix(ui): exclude @fontsource from tsdown to fix broken font paths in dist/style.css

tsdown collects all CSS imports into dist/style.css, including
@fontsource/montserrat/800.css from MainLayout.tsx. The collected
@font-face declarations reference ./files/montserrat-*.woff2 but
those files are never copied to dist/, causing downstream Next.js
builds to fail with "Module not found" when importing the CSS.

Adding @fontsource to external prevents the font CSS from being
collected, keeping dist/style.css clean (schema styles only).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Jared Scott <jared.scott@datarecce.io>

commit.message:
feat(landing): data-driven corpus snapshot with date + 24h delta

The landing page now tells a visitor, in one glance:
  1. WHAT is in the dataset (decisions, laws, graph — actual counts)
  2. WHEN it was built (explicit UTC timestamp)
  3. WHAT changed (24-hour deltas, per corpus + per court)

Changes:

docs/index.html
  - New "hero-stamp" pill directly under the big hero number:
    "As of 11 April 2026 · +147 since yesterday". Monospace,
    pill-shaped, tabular-nums — restrained, not shouty.
  - Removed the buried "+199 neu" from the hero-facts strip (it was
    invisible in practice); moved that signal up to the stamp.
  - New "Corpus snapshot" section between hero and capabilities:
    three cards (Decisions / Legislation / Graph & doctrine), each
    with current count, 24h delta badge, compositional breakdown
    (federal vs cantonal, articles indexed, statute links, etc.)
    and a single-line meta footer. Dotted separators, thin borders,
    mono numerals. Responsive (stacks on <900px).
  - Top-movers strip under the grid: top 5 courts by 24h delta,
    rendered as "BGer +34 · Genf +20 · …".
  - i18n keys added for all 5 languages (de / fr / it / rm / en):
    snapshot_heading, snapshot_decisions, snapshot_legislation,
    snapshot_graph, snapshot_federal_laws, snapshot_cantonal_laws,
    snapshot_movers, hero_stamp_asof, hero_stamp_since, and 15 more.
  - New renderCorpusSnapshot(data) populates everything from
    data.corpus, data.delta, data.generated_at. Graceful "—" when
    a sub-DB is absent (e.g. during the current cantonal crawl).

generate_stats.py
  - New collect_corpus_snapshot() reads statutes.db (federal laws +
    articles), cantonal_laws.db (cantonal laws + articles + per-
    canton counts), ok_commentaries.db (commentary count), and
    reference_graph.db (decision_citations + decision_statutes).
    Each sub-query is defensive — if a DB is missing or its schema
    differs, that field is simply omitted.
  - Extended the delta block with a "corpus" sub-key that compares
    each corpus counter against the previous stats.json.

No breaking changes to the existing hero / charts / tables — the
new section sits above them, and the existing rendering paths are
untouched. Next daily stats regen auto-populates everything.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(protocol): Version negotiation, feature flags, mandatory upgrades

Problem: No safe protocol upgrade path — bugs require hard fork
Solution: ProtocolVersion (semver, major compat), ProtocolFeatures (4 initial
flags: VM, shielded tx, sharding, PQC — height-based activation via governance),
MandatoryUpgrade (min 1000-block grace, governance-controlled). Version signed
in peer announcements. 26 tests.

commit.message:
Fix: rubric values strictly inside (0, 1) — fixes validator check

Submissions #11, #12, #13 all failed the Phase-2 "task scores out
of range" check. Previous fixes addressed runtime behavior — the
grader clamp and the inference.py stdout format — but the real
source was static metadata: every TaskDefinition in src/tasks.py
had

  rubric={"decision_correctness": 1.0, "issue_identification": 0.5,
          "review_quality": 0.5}

and 1.0 is an exact boundary value. The validator imports src.tasks
and enumerates TaskDefinition.rubric as per-task scores; the Python
code itself never reads the field, so nothing at runtime surfaced
the problem.

Updated all 9 tasks to use the grader's *actual* weights —
{decision_correctness: 0.4, issue_identification: 0.4,
review_quality: 0.2} — which sum to 1.0 (matching the hardcoded
coefficients in src/grader.py's weighted sum) and contain no
boundary values. This also makes rubric finally self-documenting:
it now describes what the grader really does instead of sitting as
misleading dead metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

for testnet. All integer math (u128 intermediate). 18 new tests.

Fix approve button failing when no Stripe payment intent exists

The capture-booking-payment edge function was called on approval even
when the payment record had no stripe_payment_intent_id or checkout
session, causing "No payment intent found" errors. Now:
1. Only call capture when a Stripe payment intent/session actually exists
2. Capture errors are non-fatal — approval proceeds with a console warning

Solution: Reputation system (0 neutral, -100 ban, +100 cap, decay toward 0),
rate limiting (50 msg/s, 30 blocks/min, 20 syncs/min), connection limits
(50 total, 30 in, 20 out). Event loop integration with 60s decay timer.
16 new tests covering flood attacks and recovery.

cleans up marker+snapshots in same WriteBatch. 10 crash simulation tests.

commit.message:
fix(security): Private key zeroization audit — 8 fixes

Problem: Keypair::drop() zeroized a temporary copy (no-op), HybridKeypair had
no Drop, intermediate key bytes in import/load/encrypt/decrypt paths leaked
Solution: Removed broken Drop (SigningKey has ZeroizeOnDrop), added unsafe
zeroing for PQC secret key, zeroize all intermediate key bytes, changed
signing_key_bytes() return to Zeroizing<[u8;32]>. 10 new tests.

commit.message:
Fix every-function crash: bundle spellcaster_core with GIMP plugin

The three shim files (_nodes.py, _architectures.py, _composites.py)
navigated ../../.. to find spellcaster_core in the repo tree. This
only works from a dev checkout — when installed into GIMP's plugin
directory the path resolves to nowhere and every workflow crashes on
import.

Fix (3 parts):
1. Shims now check for a local spellcaster_core/ in their own
   directory first, falling back to the repo tree for dev use.
2. The installer (install.py) copies spellcaster_core/ into the
   GIMP plugin directory alongside the connector files.
3. Both the auto-updater and the Repair/Update button now also
   fetch spellcaster_core/ files from GitHub, so future updates
   keep the core library in sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(framework-elevation): fulfillment handler→service, payment facades, notification thinning, conformance tests

1. handlers/fulfillment.py — FulfillmentCreateHandler e FulfillmentUpdateHandler
   delegam para services.fulfillment ao invés de reimplementar lógica.
   Remove duplicação de CARRIER_TRACKING_URLS e _enrich_tracking_url.

2. services/payment.py — adiciona get_payment_status(order) e can_cancel(order).
   Consulta Payman como fonte canônica (não order.data['payment']['status']).
   Atualiza tracking.py, pedidos.py e api/tracking.py para usar as facades.

3. handlers/notification.py — handler fica fino: lê directive, chama
   services.notification.deliver_order_notification(), escreve status.
   services/notification.py — recebe lógica extraída: _build_context,
   _qualify_template, _resolve_recipient, _resolve_backend_chain,
   deliver_order_notification().

4. tests/test_channel_config_conformance.py — 1 teste por aspecto do
   ChannelConfig (confirmation, payment, fulfillment, stock, notifications,
   pricing, editing, rules + cascade). Verifica que o runtime obedece a config.

5. tests/test_invariants.py — testes de invariante arquitetural:
   - Nenhuma view/API lê order.data['payment']['status'] diretamente
   - Fulfillment.objects.create() apenas em services/fulfillment.py
   - Handlers não criam modelos diretamente
   - lifecycle.py não tem classes Flow
   - ChannelConfig.for_channel() é chamado no lifecycle

fix(board): audit #12 — select tool bloqué en lecture seule + participant left cleanup

- _boardDown : l'outil select était exempté du check lecture seule (_brdTool!=='select')
  alors que _brdActivateSel appelle clearRect — un élève sans permission pouvait
  effacer le contenu du canvas. Toute interaction pointerdown bloquée si !_brdCanEdit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: print rewards with .4f so clamped bounds survive stdout

Submission #12 still failed the Phase-2 "task scores out of range"
check despite the grader clamping to (0.0001, 0.9999), because
inference.py printed rewards with .2f which rounded those clamped
bounds back to "0.00" and "1.00" at the stdout boundary. The
validator parses the [STEP]/[END] lines and saw the forbidden
exact-boundary values regardless of what the grader returned.

Also replaces two hardcoded "0.00" fallbacks — one in the exception
handler, one in the empty-rewards default for the [END] line — with
a constant derived from grader._EPSILON ("0.0001"), so any failure
path emits a value strictly inside (0, 1) rather than a guaranteed-
fail exact zero.

All 59 tests still pass. Verified with a live run using an invalid
API key that the exception path now prints reward=0.0001 instead of
reward=0.00.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(board): audit #11 — nettoyage complet quand un participant quitte

_brdOnParticipantLeft ne supprimait que la liste _brdParticipants :
- Curseur DOM (_brdCursor-{userId}) restait en mémoire jusqu'à fermeture
- _brdRemoteStrokes[userId] non supprimé → trait orphelin dessinable en RAF
- _brdCursorRAF et _brdCursorPt non nettoyés → RAF inutile pouvait se déclencher

Ajout du nettoyage complet : cursor DOM, remoteStrokes, RAF cancelAnimationFrame,
et pointer de dernier point.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(board): audit #10 — double-zoom overlays (curseur, curseurs distants, snap ring)

Tous les overlays positionnés en absolu dans _brdPage multipliaient par _brdZoom
alors que _brdPage lui-même est déjà CSS-transformé scale(zoom) — résultat :
position au carré (double zoom) dès que l'utilisateur zoome (pinch ou Ctrl+=).

- _brdShowEraserCursor : left/top/width/height sans * _brdZoom
- _boardSetEraserSz : width/height sans * _brdZoom
- _brdMoveRemoteCursor (RAF) : left/top sans * _brdZoom
- _brdSnapRing (indicateur ligne droite) : left/top sans * _brdZoom

À zoom=1 l'effet était invisible ; à zoom=2 tous ces éléments apparaissaient
à 4× la position attendue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(board): audit #9 — garde _brdX null + curseur gomme dès pointerdown

- Commit texte : ajout guard if(!_brdX||!_brdC) avant _brdX.save() — évite
  un TypeError si le tableau est fermé pendant une saisie active (close →
  DOM retiré → blur → setTimeout 200ms → _brdX déjà null)
- _boardDown eraser : _brdShowEraserCursor() appelé dès pointerdown sans
  attendre le premier pointermove — élimine le flash sans cercle sur premier tap

commit.message:
release: v1.7.0

Add `earos update` command for smart in-place updates of scaffolded
workspaces. Reconciles governed assets (core, profiles, overlays,
schemas, templates, skills, docs, shipped examples) against the
installed @trohde/earos version via CRLF-safe SHA-256 classification.
Interactive per-file resolution by default (keep/overwrite/diff/skip)
with --dry-run, --yes-keep-mine, --yes-overwrite, --force, and
--only <path> for CI. Non-TTY invocations without a batch flag fail
fast. Auto-regenerates earos.manifest.yaml on write.

New .earos-version marker written by init and refreshed by update
so future updates can diff against the prior installed version.

commit.message:
perf/fix(whiteboard): mémoire snap GPU, curseur gomme, reconnexion, collab sel

Mémoire (impact fort iPad Retina) :
- _brdMakeSnap() : copie canvas offscreen (GPU) au lieu de getImageData (CPU ~8MB)
- _brdRestoreSnap() : drawImage + setTransform au lieu de putImageData
- Remplace tous les getImageData/_brdSnap dans _boardDown, _boardMove, _boardUp
  et le snapshot texte live dans _boardInsertText

Curseur gomme :
- Cercle DOM (_brdEraserCursorEl) qui suit le pointeur, sized = _brdEr*_brdZoom
- cursor:none sur le canvas pendant l'effacement
- cursor:crosshair restauré au pointerup
- _brdCleanEraserCursor() appelé dans _vCloseBoard

Reconnexion socket :
- _socket.on('reconnect') ré-émet board_init ou board_join si le tableau
  était ouvert, resynchronisant l'état collaboratif après une coupure réseau

Collaboration sélection :
- _brdCommitSel() émet op {type:'snapshot'} avec le PNG de la page courante
  → les distants voient le résultat du déplacement/rotation immédiatement
- _brdApplyRemoteOp gère le nouveau type 'snapshot' via _boardRestorePage
- _boardSaveHist() non appelé pour op type snapshot (pas d'entrée hist spurious)

feat: 多項優化 — 快取、定位修復、儲存地點、PWA 離線支援

- 資料快取：首次載入存 localStorage，下次秒開再背景更新

commit.message:
fix: renomeia método de pagamento "counter" → "cash" para dinheiro em espécie

"counter" (ChannelConfig.payment.method) é config de canal: pagamento no caixa
físico, sem processamento digital. "cash" é o método específico registrado no
order.data quando o operador POS seleciona dinheiro — semanticamente mais preciso.

- pos.py: _PAYMENT_METHODS usa "cash"; default de payment_method muda para "cash"
- payment.py: skip de initiate aceita "cash" além de "counter" e "external"
- cash_register.py: filtra data__payment__method="cash" para vendas em dinheiro
- config.py: valida "cash" como método válido; documenta distinção counter vs cash

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(drift-scan): corrige regressões e atualiza terminologia — audit 2026-04-11

CRITICAL
- C1: Adiciona campo `lifecycle` (transitions, terminal_statuses) ao ChannelConfig
  para que commit.py bake corretamente o lifecycle customizado no snapshot
- C2: POS agora passa `channel_config=asdict(config)` para ModifyService e
  CommitService, espelhando o padrão de checkout.py

HIGH
- A1: Prefixos de API renomeados para persona (api/orderman/, api/offerman/,
  api/stockman/, api/craftsman/); atualizado em urls.py, tests/urls.py e
  todos os test files que usavam paths hardcoded
- A2: Hardcoded "balcao" no POS extraído para constante POS_CHANNEL_REF
  (SHOPMAN_POS_CHANNEL_REF em settings), seguindo o padrão de STOREFRONT_CHANNEL_REF
- A3: Métodos de pagamento canônicos: "dinheiro"→"counter", "cartao"→"card"
  em pos.py, payment.py e cash_register.py
- A4: Documenta hold_ids como list[dict] com {sku, hold_id, qty} em data-schemas.md
- A5: Lint fixes (ruff) nos arquivos de produção listados no audit

- M2: Adiciona stock_check_unavailable e manual_discount à tabela Session.data
- M3: kds.py e pedidos.py: customer_name lido de order.data["customer"]["name"]
  (canônico) em vez do flat "customer_name"

- production_flows.py → production_lifecycle.py
- test_flows.py → test_lifecycle.py
- test_production_flows.py → test_production_lifecycle.py
- docs/guides/flows.md → lifecycle.md (reescrito para refletir realidade)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add all-colleges directory page for internal linking and SEO

Creates /colleges as a cross-state hub linking to all 166 colleges,
with jump links, course counts, and Schema.org structured data.
Adds links from homepage header, hero, and footer. Removes legacy
/colleges → /va/colleges redirect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.2.0: 14-phase refactoring — from prototype to trusted alpha

Block policy graduation: issues carry severity (critical/major/minor),
blocking controlled by COLD_REVIEW_BLOCK_THRESHOLD (default: critical).

Ignore mechanism: .cold-review-ignore with fnmatch patterns, built-in
defaults skip lock files, build output, and minified files.

Risk-sorted diff: high-risk paths (auth, payment, db, config, api)
prioritized within token budget. No more blind head-n truncation.

Override: COLD_REVIEW_ALLOW_ONCE=1 skips block once, logged to history.

Structured failure visibility: all exit paths log state to history
(skipped/failed/passed/reported/blocked/overridden). Parse failures
marked as failed, do not block.

Schema: review JSON gains review_status, severity, confidence, category,
file. History v2 adds version, state, diff_stats.

Tests: 38 pytest tests covering schema parsing, severity thresholds,
ignore filtering, risk ranking, history format. GitHub Actions CI.

Prompt simplified ~30%: severity/category definitions added, personality
rhetoric removed. Policy enforced by code, not prompt.

README rewritten: honest about scope, documents failure modes, adoption
path, all new configuration options.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
SEO: images WebP, BlogPosting schema, BreadcrumbList, image optimizations

seo-images:
- Convert 4 blog PNG images to WebP (500-700KB → 26-76KB, avg 93% smaller)
- <picture> element with WebP source + PNG fallback on blog posts and preview
- fetchpriority="high" + decoding="async" on blog hero images
- width/height on BlogPreview images (CLS prevention)
- Update all 8 blog post frontmatter image paths to .webp

seo-schema:
- BlogPosting (was Article) on all blog posts — more specific type
- ImageObject with dimensions in BlogPosting schema
- BreadcrumbList JSON-LD on every blog post
- WebSite schema on NL + EN homepage
- Remove FAQPage JSON-LD (restricted to gov/health since Aug 2023)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add initial citation candidate backlog (1,238 papers)

Generated from OpenAlex citation graphs for 4 seed papers:
- Shuman et al. 2019 (10.1038/s41593-019-0559-0)
- Cai et al. 2016 (10.1038/nature17955)
- Zhao et al. MiniXL (10.1126/sciadv.ads4995)
- Guo et al. MiniLFOV (10.1126/sciadv.adg3918)

1,218 candidates pending analysis, 4 reviewed, 16 rejected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add citation discovery system with LLM-driven analysis pipeline

Adds a complete system for finding papers related to the lab's tools by
crawling citation graphs (OpenAlex) and analyzing full text with Claude
Code sub-agents. Papers are categorized as science, methods, software,
tool_paper, or review based on their relationship to the tools.

Pipeline: candidates/ → in-progress/ → reviewed/ or rejected/ → approved/
Pre-fetch step (fulltext.py) retrieves BibTeX and full text before analysis
so sub-agents only need file read access.

- discovery/ package: config, OpenAlex, CrossRef, Unpaywall, fulltext, candidates, analysis, approve
- .claude/commands/: analyze-citation and run-discovery skills
- tests/: 101 unit tests with mocked APIs
- CI: test-discovery.yml workflow
- Extracted shared utils (generate_key, slugify, normalize_title) into bib_utils.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(admin): use start-of-day (UTC+3) instead of rolling 24h for today metrics

Registrations/payments "today" now count from midnight Minsk time,
not from now-24h which caused yesterday's late entries to appear.
